2604.11757.title: StarVLA-$α$: Reducing Complexity in Vision-Language-Action Systems
2604.11757.paper_summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for building general-purpose robotic agents. However, the VLA landscape remains highly fragmented and complex: as existing approaches vary substantially in architectures, training data, embodiment configurations, and benchmark-specific engineering. In this work, we introduce StarVLA-$α$, a simple yet strong baseline designed to study VLA design choices under controlled conditions. StarVLA-$α$ deliberately minimizes architectural and pipeline complexity to reduce experimental confounders and enable systematic analysis. Specifically, we re-evaluate several key design axes, including action modeling strategies, robot-specific pretraining, and interface engineering. Across unified multi-benchmark training on LIBERO, SimplerEnv, RoboTwin, and RoboCasa, the same simple baseline remains highly competitive, indicating that a strong VLM backbone combined with minimal design is already sufficient to achieve strong performance without relying on additional architectural complexity or engineering tricks. Notably, our single generalist model outperforms $π_{0.5}$ by 20\% on the public real-world RoboChallenge benchmark. We expect StarVLA-$α$ to serve as a solid starting point for future research in the VLA regime. Code will be released at https://github.com/starVLA/starVLA.
2604.11757.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型最近作為建立通用機器人代理的有前景範式而出現。然而，VLA 的生態系統仍然高度分散且複雜：現有的方法在架構、訓練數據、具身配置和基準特定工程方面差異顯著。在這項工作中，我們介紹了 StarVLA-$α$，這是一個簡單但強大的基線，旨在在受控條件下研究 VLA 設計選擇。StarVLA-$α$ 有意最小化架構和流程的複雜性，以減少實驗干擾並促進系統分析。具體而言，我們重新評估了幾個關鍵設計軸，包括行動建模策略、機器人特定的預訓練和介面工程。在 LIBERO、SimplerEnv、RoboTwin 和 RoboCasa 上的統一多基準訓練中，相同的簡單基線仍然具有高度競爭力，這表明強大的 VLM 骨幹結合最小設計已經足以在不依賴額外架構複雜性或工程技巧的情況下實現強勁表現。值得注意的是，我們的單一通才模型在公共現實世界 RoboChallenge 基準上超越了 $π_{0.5}$ 20\%。我們期望 StarVLA-$α$ 能夠作為未來 VLA 領域研究的堅實起點。代碼將在 https://github.com/starVLA/starVLA 上發布。
2604.11757.author: Jinhui Ye et.al.
2604.11757.authors: Jinhui Ye, Ning Gao, Senqiao Yang, Jinliang Zheng, Zixuan Wang, Yuxin Chen, Pengguang Chen, Yilun Chen, Shu Liu, Jiaya Jia
